what: Search for the required sample size
title: Getting started with anovapowersim
text: Use power_n() search sample size needed reach requested power. example mixed design one two-level -subject factor (cond) one four-level within-subject factor (stim). tests cond:stim interaction 90% power detect partial eta squared 0.14. power_n() reports required number subjects per -subject cell corresponding total N. purely within-subject design, reported n_per_cell total sample size. output table uses compact column names: num_df den_df ANOVA degrees freedom, ncp noncentrality parameter, power_calc calculated noncentral-F power, power_sim simulation estimate. example uses 1000 simulations speed. Use least 5000 simulations stable estimate; package default 10000.
code: power_n(   between = c(cond = 2), # cond has 2 levels   within = c(stim = 4), # stim has 4 levels   term = "cond:stim",   target_pes = 0.14,   alpha = 0.05,   power = 0.90,   n_sims = 1000, # use 5000+ for a more precise estimate   seed = 123 # for reproducibility ) #> <anovapowersim_curve> #>   term:          'cond:stim' #>   target power:  0.900 #>   alpha:         0.05 #>   effect size:   pes = 0.14 #>   n values:      6 per-cell sample sizes visited #>   sims per cell size: 1000 #>   SS type:       III #>   n needed for between-subjects cell: 17 #>   total N needed: 34 #>  #>  n_per_cell total_n n_sims num_df den_df    ncp power_calc power_sim #>          13      26   1000      3     72 11.721      0.808     0.795 #>          16      32   1000      3     90 14.651      0.897     0.885 #>          17      34   1000      3     96 15.628      0.917     0.912 #>          18      36   1000      3    102 16.605      0.934     0.936 #>          20      40   1000      3    114 18.558      0.958     0.946 #>          26      52   1000      3    150 24.419      0.991     0.991

title: Covariance and nonsphericity
text: Cell names combine within-factor levels underscores. within = c(time = 3, condition = 2), expected names time1_condition1, time1_condition2, time2_condition1, time2_condition2, time3_condition1, time3_condition2. Correlation names join pair cells :. Unlisted measurements use default_sd, unlisted pairs use default_correlation.
code: covariance <- within_covariance(   default_sd = 1,   default_correlation = 0.5,   standard_deviations = c(     "time3_condition2" = 1.2   ),   correlations = c(     "time1_condition1:time1_condition2" = 0.6,     "time2_condition1:time2_condition2" = 0.7   ) )

title: Covariance and nonsphericity
text: Pass object balanced simulation function: custom covariance structure calibrates target effect generates every simulated dataset. package projects covariance tested term’s contrast space calculates population Greenhouse–Geisser epsilon. power_calc applies epsilon degrees freedom noncentrality. population epsilon 1, power_sim uses simulated dataset’s sample-estimated Greenhouse–Geisser-adjusted p-value sums-- squares type II III. Type tests remain uncorrected issue warning. Huynh–Feldt corrections currently implemented.
code: power_n(   within = c(time = 3, condition = 2),   term = "time:condition",   target_pes = 0.14,   power = 0.90,   n_sims = 5000,   covariance = covariance,   seed = 123 )

title: Power for unbalanced designs
text: Define every cell cell_design(). Factor values identify cell, n number subjects -subject group, m population mean, sd marginal population standard deviation. Name repeated- measures factors within; remaining factors -subject factors. repeated-measures designs, n must appear every within-subject row belonging given -subject group. means define complete effect pattern, including main effects interactions. Standard deviations can differ across groups within-subject cells. Every factor must least two observed levels, every factor-level combination must defined exactly . cells missing, cell_design() reports ones. Supply default_n, default_m, default_sd together fill missing cells automatically:
code: unbalanced_design <- cell_design(   group = "control",   time = "pre",  n = 22, m = 10.0, sd = 2.0,   group = "control",   time = "post", n = 22, m = 11.0, sd = 2.2,   group = "treatment", time = "pre",  n = 31, m = 10.1, sd = 2.4,   group = "treatment", time = "post", n = 31, m = 12.4, sd = 2.8,   within = "time" ) cell_design(   group = "control",   time = "pre",  n = 22, m = 10.0, sd = 2.0,   group = "control",   time = "post", n = 22, m = 11.0, sd = 2.2,   group = "treatment", time = "pre",  n = 31, m = 10.1, sd = 2.4,   within = "time",   default_n = 31, default_m = 12.4, default_sd = 2.8 )

title: Power for unbalanced designs
text: repeated measures, use unbalanced_covariance() define correlations. deliberately standard-deviation argument: power_unbalanced() combines correlations cell-specific sd values construct covariance matrix -subject group. one within-subject factor, pair names use levels, "pre:post".
code: power_unbalanced(   design = unbalanced_design,   term = "group:time",   covariance = unbalanced_covariance(     default_correlation = 0.5,     correlations = c("pre:post" = 0.7)   ),   n_sims = 5000,   parallel = TRUE,   seed = 123 )

title: Power for unbalanced designs
text: result reports simulated power, partial eta squared deterministic reference dataset matching design assumptions, mean, median, 95% interval partial eta squared across successful simulations. effect-size summaries describe exact allocation, means, variances, correlations, tested term, sums--squares type supplied user.

title: Power for unbalanced designs

code: multi_within_design <- cell_design(   group = "A", time = "pre",  cond = "control", n = 10, m = 0.0, sd = 1,   group = "A", time = "pre",  cond = "treat",   n = 10, m = 0.5, sd = 1,   group = "A", time = "post", cond = "control", n = 10, m = 0.2, sd = 1,   group = "A", time = "post", cond = "treat",   n = 10, m = 1.0, sd = 1,   group = "B", time = "pre",  cond = "control", n = 15, m = 0.0, sd = 1,   group = "B", time = "pre",  cond = "treat",   n = 15, m = 0.6, sd = 1,   group = "B", time = "post", cond = "control", n = 15, m = 0.3, sd = 1,   group = "B", time = "post", cond = "treat",   n = 15, m = 1.4, sd = 1,   within = c("time", "cond") )  power_unbalanced(   design = multi_within_design,   term = "group:time:cond",   covariance = unbalanced_covariance(     correlations = c("pre_control:post_control" = 0.6)   ),   n_sims = 5000,   seed = 123 )

what: anovapowersim: Design-Based Power Simulation for Factorial ANOVA — anovapowersim-package
title: anovapowersim: Design-Based Power Simulation for Factorial ANOVA — anovapowersim-package
text: Simulation-based power analysis balanced factorial ANOVA designs. Given - within-subject factor structures, target partial eta squared, sample sizes, anovapowersim generates default term-specific cell means, simulates datasets using default custom within-subject covariance structures, refits ANOVA stats, returns tidy power estimates ggplot2 power curve.

title: anovapowersim: Design-Based Power Simulation for Factorial ANOVA — anovapowersim-package
text: main entry points power_n() required sample size, power_achieved() achieved power, power_sensitivity() minimum detectable effect size, power_curve() power across explicit sample sizes. power_unbalanced() provides experimental means-based simulation one fixed unbalanced design. Experimental calculation-counterparts provided power_n_calc(), power_achieved_calc(), power_sensitivity_calc(). lower-level building blocks also exported users can compose custom simulations: balanced_anova_design() design_term_means() simulate_design_dataset() within_covariance() cell_design() unbalanced_covariance() compute_scale_factor()

title: Compute the mean-deviation scaling factor from a change in partial eta squared — compute_scale_factor
text: single positive numeric value k. k > 1 amplifies effect, k < 1 shrinks , k == 1 leaves unchanged.

title: Compute the mean-deviation scaling factor from a change in partial eta squared — compute_scale_factor
text: derivation straightforward: partial eta squared can written pes = SS_effect / (SS_effect + C), C part denominator held fixed package's rescaling. Thus pes / (1 - pes) scales target effect's sum squares. Scaling term's deviations k scales target effect's sum squares k^2, required multiplier $$k = \sqrt{\frac{p_{\mathrm{new}} / (1 - p_{\mathrm{new}})}                       {p_{\mathrm{old}} / (1 - p_{\mathrm{old}})}}.$$

title: Calculate ANOVA effect-size sensitivity at a fixed sample size — power_sensitivity_calc
text: Named integer vector -subject factor level counts, e.g. c(group = 2). Use NULL -subject factors. within Named integer vector within-subject factor level counts, e.g. c(time = 3, condition = 4). Use NULL within-subject factors. term Character scalar naming ANOVA term test, e.g. "group:time". Interaction terms order-insensitive; "time:group" resolves "group:time" design's factor order. n Number subjects per -subject cell. purely within-subject design, total sample size. power Desired target power. alpha Significance threshold. pes_min Lower bound partial eta-squared search interval. pes_max Upper bound partial eta-squared search interval. pes_tol Maximum width final calculated partial eta-squared bracket. gpower Logical; TRUE, use GPower-style noncentrality convention lambda = total_n * f^2. default FALSE uses lambda = den_df * f^2. term whose within-subject component one degree freedom, gpower's target_pes equal partial eta squared actually achieved (mirrors property GPower's "Cohen (1988)" repeated-measures convention, adjust number measurements); warning issued case. Use default want target_pes match reported expected partial eta squared exactly. epsilon Population nonsphericity correction within-subject component term. Must lie theoretical lower bound 1 / within_term_df 1. default 1 assumes sphericity. Values 1 multiply numerator degrees freedom, denominator degrees freedom, noncentrality parameter. Nonsphericity corrections apply purely -subject terms.

title: anovapowersim (development version)
text: Added experimental, development-version-cell_design(), unbalanced_covariance(), power_unbalanced() functions simulation-power analysis fixed unbalanced allocation user-defined cell means, marginal standard deviations, within-subject correlations. Results include simulated power partial eta-squared diagnostics, deliberately omit calculated power. power_unbalanced() now derives worst-case population Greenhouse–Geisser epsilon across -subject cells tested term, reports $epsilon, bases power_sim Greenhouse–Geisser-corrected simulated p-value whenever epsilon 1 (requires ss_type "III" "II"; warning issued ss_type = "" combined non-spherical design). Breaking (experimental): cell_design() now takes within argument (character vector within-subject factor names, NULL) stores returned design; power_unbalanced() longer accepts within reads design instead. Move within = ... power_unbalanced() cell_design(). cell_design() gained default_n, default_m, default_sd. Supply three auto-fill missing cells complete factorial design; supplying three error, supplying none requires every cell defined explicitly (). cell_design() now reports exact missing factor-level combinations design incomplete, instead count, errors clearly factor fewer two observed levels (previously surfaced later, inside power_unbalanced(), unhelpful low-level contrast-fitting error). within-subject n-consistency check (n identical across within-subject rows -subject cell) now runs cell_design() construction time; previously surfaced inside power_unbalanced(). Added experimental, development-version-power_achieved() function simulation-based achieved-power estimation fixed sample size partial eta squared. Added experimental, development-version-power_sensitivity() function simulation-based minimum-detectable partial eta-squared searches fixed sample size target power. Added experimental, development-version-power_achieved_calc() power_sensitivity_calc() functions equivalent fixed-sample analyses using calculated noncentral-F power without simulations. Added power_n_calc() calculated-power, simulation-free sample-size searches balanced ANOVA designs. Added epsilon argument power_n_calc() calculated-power nonsphericity corrections terms containing within-subject factors. Added within_covariance() covariance argument power_n() power_curve() simulations can use custom within-subject covariance structures. functions now derive term-specific population Greenhouse–Geisser epsilon covariance apply calculated power. power_curve(), power_n(), power_achieved(), power_sensitivity(), power_n_calc(), power_achieved_calc(), power_sensitivity_calc(), design_term_means() now warn gpower = TRUE combined term whose within-subject component one degree freedom (.e. within factor two levels). case target_pes gpower = TRUE equal partial eta squared actually achieved – mirrors property GPower’s “Cohen (1988)” repeated-measures convention, adjust number measurements, rather bug package (gpower = TRUE remains exact replica GPower’s noncentrality formula). Use default gpower = FALSE target_pes match reported expected partial eta squared exactly. supplied covariance yields population Greenhouse–Geisser epsilon 1, power_curve(), power_n(), power_achieved(), power_sensitivity() now base power_sim simulated dataset’s Greenhouse–Geisser-corrected p-value instead uncorrected univariate test, power_sim power_calc estimate corrected test rather diverging non-sphericity. correction requires ss_type "III" "II"; "", simulated p-values remain uncorrected, functions now warn ss_type = "" combined covariance whose derived epsilon 1.
